_doc: Global synthesis control parameters for physics_synth.py::synthesize_note(). All values here override the defaults. Per-note extracted data lives in params.json.

render._doc: Passed directly to synthesize_note(). All are freely adjustable at any time, including in real-time.
render._duration_doc: Note duration in seconds. null = use duration_s from params.json (up to 8s).
render._fade_out_doc: Fade-out duration at note end, in seconds.
render._target_rms_doc: Output RMS level (0.0-1.0). Controls overall loudness before clipping.

timbre._doc: Spectral and harmonic shaping. Each parameter is a single float — safe to vary in real-time.
timbre._harmonic_brightness_doc: Boosts amplitude of upper partials: gain = 1 + brightness * log2(k). 0=flat, 1=moderate boost, 2=strong. Compensates for extraction underestimating high-k transient energy.
timbre._beat_scale_doc: Scales inter-string beating frequency. 0=no beating (static), 1=extracted value, 2=twice as fast beating. Affects 'liveliness' and modulation depth.
timbre._eq_strength_doc: Blend of spectral EQ correction (LTASE_orig/LTASE_synth ratio). 0=off, 1=full. Currently problematic: EQ was computed including room acoustics so it distorts spectral shape. Leave at 0 until re-derived.
timbre._soundboard_strength_doc: Modal IR convolution simulating soundboard resonance. Parked at 0 — causes 'croaking' band-pass distortion with current 40-mode IR.

stereo._doc: Spatial parameters. Safe to vary in real-time.
stereo._pan_spread_doc: Per-string pan angle spread in radians. 0=mono center, 0.55=natural spread, 1.5=wide. Bass notes are panned left, treble right (±0.20 rad global offset).
stereo._stereo_boost_doc: M/S stereo width multiplier on top of sample-derived width_factor. 1.0=use derived factor only, 2.0=double the side channel.
stereo._eq_stereo_width_doc: Whether to apply per-note stereo_width_factor from spectral_eq (derived from rms(S_orig)/rms(M_orig) vs synth). true=on.

per_note_overrides._doc: Optional per-MIDI overrides applied on top of extracted params.json values. Format: midi_note -> parameter -> value. These are NOT yet implemented in synthesize_note() but show intended extension.

example_A2_45._tau_scale_k1_doc: Scale tau1 of k=1 partial. A2 k=1 has extracted tau=0.29s which is too short. Multiply by 2.0 to get 0.58s.

extraction_params._doc: Parameters used during extract_params.py. Changing these requires re-running extraction.
extraction_params._inharmonicity_doc: f_k = k * f0 * sqrt(1 + B*k^2). B extracted per-note.
extraction_params._biexp_doc: Fit bi-exponential (a1*exp(-t/tau1) + (1-a1)*exp(-t/tau2)) if residual from mono-exp exceeds threshold.
